criteria_verification.criterion_text: Refuses any stage that is not in `{PRODUCT_OWNER, ARCHITECT}`
criteria_verification.evidence: Function refuses early with ValueError naming the offending stage when stage not in SPECIALIST_CAPABILITY_BY_STAGE (which keys on PRODUCT_OWNER and ARCHITECT only). TestStageRefusal parametrises across the six other StageClass members and verifies (a) ValueError is raised, (b) no dispatch is attempted, (c) no stage_log row is written.
criteria_verification.notes: Function refuses early with ValueError naming the offending stage when stage not in SPECIALIST_CAPABILITY_BY_STAGE (which keys on PRODUCT_OWNER and ARCHITECT only). criteria_verification.evidence: dispatch_specialist_stage calls forward_context_builder.build_for(stage=stage, build_id=build_id, feature_id=feature_id) and threads each returned ContextEntry into the DispatchParameter list as 'context' params. TestForwardContextWiring verifies the architect dispatch carries the product-owner approved charter text in its parameter values, and that the product-owner dispatch (entry stage) carries no forward context.
criteria_verification.notes: dispatch_specialist_stage calls forward_context_builder.build_for(stage=stage, build_id=build_id, feature_id=feature_id) and threads each returned ContextEntry into the DispatchParameter list as 'context' params. criteria_verification.criterion_text: Delegates to FEAT-FORGE-003's specialist-dispatch tool with the
criteria_verification.evidence: SPECIALIST_CAPABILITY_BY_STAGE maps PRODUCT_OWNER -> 'product_owner_specialist' and ARCHITECT -> 'architect_specialist'; dispatcher passes the mapped capability to dispatch_surface.dispatch(). TestCapabilityRouting parametrises both stages and asserts surface.last_call['capability'] equals the expected name and that SPECIALIST_CAPABILITY_BY_STAGE[stage] matches.
criteria_verification.notes: SPECIALIST_CAPABILITY_BY_STAGE maps PRODUCT_OWNER -> 'product_owner_specialist' and ARCHITECT -> 'architect_specialist'; dispatcher passes the mapped capability to dispatch_surface.dispatch(). criteria_verification.criterion_text: Threads the build's `correlation_id` through the dispatch envelope
criteria_verification.evidence: _build_dispatch_parameters always emits a non-sensitive 'correlation_id' DispatchParameter as the first entry, value threaded unchanged from the function argument. TestCorrelationIdThreading asserts exactly one such parameter exists, value matches input verbatim, and sensitive=False so persistence does not redact it.
criteria_verification.notes: _build_dispatch_parameters always emits a non-sensitive 'correlation_id' DispatchParameter as the first entry, value threaded unchanged from the function argument. TestCorrelationIdThreading asserts exactly one such parameter exists, value matches input verbatim, and sensitive=False so persistence does not redact it.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Records the dispatch as a `stage_log` entry on submit and updates on
criteria_verification.evidence: Defined StageLogWriter Protocol with two methods: record_dispatch_submit (returns entry_id) and record_dispatch_reply. Dispatcher calls submit before delegating to FEAT-FORGE-003 and reply after translating the outcome, threading coach_score / criterion_breakdown / detection_findings / reason. TestStageLogLifecycle asserts (a) one submit row with capability + correlation_id, (b) one reply row carrying the parsed Coach score (0.91) + criterion breakdown + findings keyed to the same entry_id, and (c) write-before-send: an observing dispatch surface sees one submit row already present at dispatch time.
criteria_verification.notes: Defined StageLogWriter Protocol with two methods: record_dispatch_submit (returns entry_id) and record_dispatch_reply. Dispatcher calls submit before delegating to FEAT-FORGE-003 and reply after translating the outcome, threading coach_score / criterion_breakdown / detection_findings / reason. TestStageLogLifecycle asserts (a) one submit row with capability + correlation_id, (b) one reply row carrying the parsed Coach score (0.91) + criterion breakdown + findings keyed to the same entry_id, and (c) write-before-send: an observing dispatch surface sees one submit row already present at dispatch time.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: On degraded specialist (no healthy specialist on cache), returns a
criteria_verification.evidence: _translate_outcome maps Degraded -> StageDispatchOutcome.DEGRADED, copying outcome.reason through. StageDispatchResult re-exports the enum members as ClassVar attributes so AC-007's literal access pattern (StageDispatchResult.DEGRADED) works. TestDegradedPath seeds a Degraded reply with reason='no_specialist_resolvable' and asserts result.outcome is StageDispatchResult.DEGRADED and the writer records the same outcome + reason.
criteria_verification.notes: _translate_outcome maps Degraded -> StageDispatchOutcome.DEGRADED, copying outcome.reason through. StageDispatchResult re-exports the enum members as ClassVar attributes so AC-007's literal access pattern (StageDispatchResult.DEGRADED) works. TestDegradedPath seeds a Degraded reply with reason='no_specialist_resolvable' and asserts result.outcome is StageDispatchResult.DEGRADED and the writer records the same outcome + reason.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Unit test mocking FEAT-FORGE-003 dispatch surface: success path,
criteria_verification.evidence: Three dedicated test classes use the in-memory RecordingDispatchSurface fake (which structurally satisfies SpecialistDispatchSurface, asserted in TestProtocolConformance): TestSuccessPath (SyncResult -> COMPLETED with coach_score=0.78), TestDegradedPath (Degraded -> DEGRADED), TestSoftTimeoutPath (DispatchError(error_explanation='local_timeout') -> SOFT_TIMEOUT, plus a non-timeout error variant -> ERROR). TestSoftTimeoutPath also asserts the dispatcher does NOT retry (call_count == 1) -- retry-with-context lives at the supervisor layer per ASSUM-005.
criteria_verification.notes: Three dedicated test classes use the in-memory RecordingDispatchSurface fake (which structurally satisfies SpecialistDispatchSurface, asserted in TestProtocolConformance): TestSuccessPath (SyncResult -> COMPLETED with coach_score=0.78), TestDegradedPath (Degraded -> DEGRADED), TestSoftTimeoutPath (DispatchError(error_explanation='local_timeout') -> SOFT_TIMEOUT, plus a non-timeout error variant -> ERROR). TestSoftTimeoutPath also asserts the dispatcher does NOT retry (call_count == 1) -- retry-with-context lives at the supervisor layer per ASSUM-005.

issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)
No seam tests detected for cross-boundary feature. Consider adding seam/contract/boundary tests to validate technology boundary interactions. This is a recommendation, not a blocking gate.

rationale: All quality gates passed. Independent verification confirmed. All acceptance criteria met. Architecture context: 272 chars provided.
